primary_argument: G3B.SI is exhibiting momentum exhaustion at its 10-day high with negligible upside participation while the core portfolio chain rallies, proving the diversification thesis is structurally fragile and vulnerable to immediate mean reversion.
supporting_evidence: Price sits exactly at the 10-day high ($5.47) with a zero-session momentum streak and only +0.33% daily gain despite +2.97% to +5.66% surges across NVDA, AVGO, SMH, and QQQ; volume confirms distribution rather than accumulation at 424,798 shares (1.25x the 30-day average), showing a complete lack of institutional conviction at resistance; Singapore police seized a $42M property in an Nvidia chip smuggling probe, signaling escalating regulatory friction and capital control risks that directly threaten the stability of the local banking sector underpinning this ETF.
key_assumption: The bull assumes SGD-denominated banking dividends provide reliable portfolio cushioning against US volatility, but ignores that MAS policy shifts or capital flight pressures will compress DBS/OCBC/UOB net interest margins and force correlation convergence during stress events, destroying the diversification premise.
regime_sensitivity: Strengthens in a high_vix crisis regime because global liquidity contraction forces SGD assets and US tech to sell off simultaneously, while MAS may be compelled to intervene or cut rates, collapsing the dividend yield thesis.
watch_items: G3B.SI closes below $5.28 (10-day low) on volume exceeding 600,000 shares
SGD/USD breaks below 1.32 or MAS announces emergency banking rate intervention
